text.title: llm-observability-stack-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for llm-observability-stack-2026 as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Deploying observability tooling without baseline metrics, then immediately tuning alerts to the noisy production traffic.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Observability is valuable only relative to a baseline. Without 7-14 days of production traffic history, you cannot distinguish normal variance from real degradation, and alerts will trigger on noise, causing alert fatigue.

failure-mode.description: Focusing cost monitoring exclusively and ignoring quality metrics; discovering too late that cost optimizations (cheaper models, higher temperatures, shorter prompts) degraded output quality.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cost and quality are coupled; cost reductions often trade quality. Monitoring must track both simultaneously. A 20% cost reduction that halves quality is a net loss.

failure-mode.description: Using P95/P99 latency from the provider's own metrics without confirming it includes your proxy/instrumentation overhead.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Helicone, Langfuse, and other observability tools add latency; provider metrics often do not include this overhead. You must measure end-to-end latency from your application's perspective.

failure-mode.description: Running Langfuse evaluation (LLM-as-judge) at 100% sample rate to "maximize quality insight" and then being surprised by evaluation costs approaching 50% of production cost.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Evaluation is expensive because it makes LLM calls. Sampling at 5-10% is sufficient for trend detection and statistical significance while keeping cost linear with production traffic.

failure-mode.description: Propagating user_id or customer_id to observability backends without explicit consent from legal/security, then having the vendor flag sensitive data in their systems.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Some observability vendors log personally identifiable information by default; self-hosted solutions (Langfuse, Phoenix) avoid this. Coordinate with security and legal before attaching PII to traces.
